key_insight: LLM personas have two distinct parts: stable aggregate trait scores and a separate geometry of correlated responses that depends on question framing.
engineering_relevance: For reliable persona evaluation, systems should measure both summary scores and frame-sensitive correlation structure, especially when prompt order or context can change.
method: Built within-instance correlation matrices from IPIP-50 questionnaire responses.
Compared geometry on symmetric positive definite (SPD) manifolds under different question orderings.
Tested GPT-4o with American and Chinese-American persona settings plus randomized vs shared frames.
evidence_signal: Aggregate Big Five features dropped 21% under randomization but remained frame-robust, while geometric SPD features fell 42% under frame misalignment and recovered to 84% with shared frames.
watch_item: Results may depend on the specific questionnaire, personas, and model/prompt setup, so the frame-dependent geometry should be validated across other instruments and LLMs.
paper_title: The Dual Nature of LLM Persona: Aggregated Tendencies and Frame-Dependent Geometry
